```{r}
    TrophicIndex = as.numeric(TrophicIndex),
    TrophicGroup = as.character(TrophicGroup)
  ) %>%
  arrange(TrophicIndex)

write_csv(lake_retained_tbl_obs, "LakeZurich_RetainedSpecies_TrophicIndex_TrophicGroup_OBS.csv")

# Hypothesis-testing readiness: KW + Cliff's delta (extremes)
predictors <- lake_g %>% select(where(is.numeric)) %>% names()
predictors <- setdiff(predictors, c("TrophicIndex"))

kw_tbl <- lapply(predictors, function(v) {
  dat <- lake_g %>% select(TrophicGroup, all_of(v)) %>% filter(!is.na(TrophicGroup), !is.na(.data[[v]]))
  kt <- kruskal.test(dat[[v]] ~ dat$TrophicGroup)
  tibble(Predictor = v, chi_sq = unname(kt$statistic), df = unname(kt$parameter), p_value = unname(kt$p.value))
}) %>% bind_rows() %>%
  mutate(p_adj_BH = p.adjust(p_value, method = "BH"))

cliffs_delta <- function(x, y) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]; y <- y[is.finite(y)]
  if (length(x) == 0 || length(y) == 0) return(NA_real_)
  diffs <- outer(x, y, "-")
  (sum(diffs > 0) - sum(diffs < 0)) / (length(x) * length(y))
}
cliff_mag <- function(d) {
  ad <- abs(d)
  dplyr::case_when(
    is.na(ad)  ~ NA_character_,
    ad < 0.147 ~ "negligible",
    ad < 0.33  ~ "small",
    ad < 0.474 ~ "medium",
    TRUE       ~ "large"
  )
}

cliff_tbl <- lapply(predictors, function(v) {
  dat <- lake_g %>% select(TrophicGroup, all_of(v)) %>%
    filter(!is.na(TrophicGroup), !is.na(.data[[v]]), TrophicGroup %in% c("Oligo_like","Copio_like"))
  x <- dat %>% filter(TrophicGroup == "Oligo_like") %>% pull(v)
  y <- dat %>% filter(TrophicGroup == "Copio_like") %>% pull(v)
  d <- cliffs_delta(x, y)
  tibble(Predictor = v, Cliff_delta = d, Magnitude = cliff_mag(d))
}) %>% bind_rows()

stats_summary_tbl_obs <- kw_tbl %>%
  transmute(Predictor, KW_chi2 = chi_sq, KW_df = df, KW_p = p_value, KW_p_BH = p_adj_BH) %>%
  left_join(cliff_tbl, by = "Predictor") %>%
  mutate(
    Cliff_delta = round(as.numeric(Cliff_delta), 3),
    KW_p        = signif(as.numeric(KW_p), 3),
    KW_p_BH     = signif(as.numeric(KW_p_BH), 3)
  ) %>%
  arrange(KW_p_BH)

write_csv(stats_summary_tbl_obs, "LakeZurich_TrophicIndex_PredictorStats_Summary_OBS.csv")
# =============================================================================
```
```{r}
# =============================================================================
# BLOCK 10B — MONEY FIGURE (ESTIMATED Lake Zurich projection) + exports
# Same as 10A, but uses lake_scored_est and labels everything "EST".
#
# Inputs assumed to exist:
#   train_scored
#   lake_scored_est
#   trophic_cols (optional)
#
# Exports:
#   MoneyFigure_EST.pdf
#   LakeZurich_RetainedSpecies_TrophicIndex_TrophicGroup_EST.csv
#   LakeZurich_TrophicIndex_PredictorStats_Summary_EST.csv
# =============================================================================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(ggrepel)
  library(patchwork)
  library(readr)
})

# 1) Training-anchored cutoffs (same reference thresholds)
q25_train <- unname(quantile(train_scored$TrophicIndex, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE))
q75_train <- unname(quantile(train_scored$TrophicIndex, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE))

cat("\n[EST] Training-anchored cutoffs:\n")
cat("  Q25 =", round(q25_train, 4), "\n")
cat("  Q75 =", round(q75_train, 4), "\n")

assign_groups <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(
      TrophicGroup = case_when(
        TrophicIndex <= q25_train ~ "Oligo_like",
        TrophicIndex >= q75_train ~ "Copio_like",
        TRUE                      ~ "Intermediate"
      ),
      TrophicGroup = factor(TrophicGroup, levels = c("Oligo_like","Intermediate","Copio_like"))
    )
}

train_g <- assign_groups(train_scored)
lake_g  <- assign_groups(lake_scored_est)

cat("\n[EST] Group sizes (training):\n")
print(train_g %>% count(TrophicGroup))
cat("\n[EST] Group sizes (lake):\n")
print(lake_g %>% count(TrophicGroup))

group_cols <- c(
  Oligo_like   = "#2166AC",
  Intermediate = "grey60",
  Copio_like   = "#B2182B"
)

# Panel A — Density overlay (Training vs Lake EST)
pA <- ggplot() +
  geom_density(data = train_g, aes(TrophicIndex, color = "Training"), linewidth = 1) +
  geom_density(data = lake_g,  aes(TrophicIndex, color = "LakeZurich_EST"), linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(q25_train, q75_train), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(Training = "black", LakeZurich_EST = "grey50")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "A) Calibration + occupancy of index space",
    subtitle = "Lake Zurich projection uses ESTIMATED (completion-corrected) traits (dashed = training Q25/Q75)",
    x = "TrophicIndex (training PC1 score)",
    y = "Density",
    color = NULL
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

# Panel B — Lake EST density colored by training-anchored groups
fmt_counts <- function(df) {
  tab <- df %>% count(TrophicGroup)
  paste0(tab$TrophicGroup, "=", tab$n, collapse = " | ")
}
sub_lake <- paste0("n: ", fmt_counts(lake_g), "   (Q25=", round(q25_train,2), ", Q75=", round(q75_train,2), ")")

pB <- ggplot(lake_g, aes(TrophicIndex, fill = TrophicGroup, color = TrophicGroup)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.35, linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(q25_train, q75_train), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "B) Lake Zurich (EST) with training-anchored thresholds",
    subtitle = sub_lake,
    x = "TrophicIndex (training PC1 score)",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

# Panel C — Ranked axis with training anchors
use_class_colors <- ("Classification" %in% names(train_g)) && exists("trophic_cols")
comb <- bind_rows(
  lake_g  %>% transmute(Species_ID, TrophicIndex, Dataset = "LakeZurich_EST",
                        Classification = NA_character_, Label = NA_character_),
  train_g %>% transmute(
    Species_ID = as.character(Species_ID),
    TrophicIndex,
    Dataset = "Training",
    Classification = if ("Classification" %in% names(train_g)) as.character(Classification) else NA_character_,
    Label = if ("Label" %in% names(train_g)) as.character(Label) else as.character(Species_ID)
  )
) %>% arrange(TrophicIndex) %>% mutate(Rank = row_number())

pC <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(
    data = comb %>% filter(Dataset == "LakeZurich_EST"),
    aes(Rank, TrophicIndex),
    color = "grey75", alpha = 0.8, size = 1.8
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = comb %>% filter(Dataset == "Training"),
    aes(Rank, TrophicIndex, color = if (use_class_colors) Classification else Dataset),
    size = 2.8
  ) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = comb %>% filter(Dataset == "Training" & !is.na(Label)),
    aes(Rank, TrophicIndex, label = Label,
        color = if (use_class_colors) Classification else Dataset),
    size = 3,
    max.overlaps = Inf,
    box.padding = 0.35,
    point.padding = 0.15,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(q25_train, q75_train), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  { if (use_class_colors) scale_color_manual(values = trophic_cols) else scale_color_manual(values = c(Training = "black")) } +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "C) Ranked axis with training anchors",
    subtitle = "Grey = Lake Zurich (EST); labeled points = indicator species (dashed = Q25/Q75)",
    x = "Rank along combined trophic index (low → high)",
    y = "TrophicIndex (training PC1 score)",
    color = NULL
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

# Panel D — Bootstrap stability + near-boundary fraction
set.seed(123)
B <- 2000
boot_cutoffs <- replicate(B, {
  idx <- sample(seq_len(nrow(train_g)), replace = TRUE)
  x <- train_g$TrophicIndex[idx]
  c(q25 = unname(quantile(x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)),
    q75 = unname(quantile(x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)))
}) %>% t() %>% as.data.frame()

ci_q25 <- quantile(boot_cutoffs$q25, c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
ci_q75 <- quantile(boot_cutoffs$q75, c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)

eps <- 0.1 * IQR(train_g$TrophicIndex, na.rm = TRUE)
near_boundary <- lake_g %>%
  mutate(near = (abs(TrophicIndex - q25_train) <= eps) | (abs(TrophicIndex - q75_train) <= eps))
near_frac <- mean(near_boundary$near, na.rm = TRUE)
near_n <- sum(near_boundary$near, na.rm = TRUE)

boot_long <- boot_cutoffs %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "Cutoff", values_to = "Value") %>%
  mutate(Cutoff = recode(Cutoff, q25 = "Q25", q75 = "Q75"))

pD <- ggplot(boot_long, aes(Value, fill = Cutoff)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 35, alpha = 0.55, color = "grey30") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = q25_train, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = q75_train, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "D) Cutoff stability (bootstrap on training set)",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "EST projection; 95% CI: Q25[", round(ci_q25[1],2), ", ", round(ci_q25[2],2),
      "], Q75[", round(ci_q75[1],2), ", ", round(ci_q75[2],2),
      "]; Lake near-boundary=", near_n, "/", nrow(lake_g), " (", round(100*near_frac,1), "%), eps=", round(eps,2)
    ),
    x = "Bootstrap cutoff value",
    y = "Count",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

money_figure_est <- (pA | pB) / (pC | pD) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Training-defined genomic trophic index (Lake Zurich EST projection)",
    subtitle = "Index + thresholds defined in indicator species; applied unchanged to Lake Zurich taxa"
  )

print(money_figure_est)

# -----------------------------
# EXPORTS (EST)
# -----------------------------
ggsave("MoneyFigure_EST.pdf", money_figure_est, width = 13, height = 10, units = "in")

lake_retained_tbl_est <- lake_g %>%
  transmute(
    Species_ID = as.character(Species_ID),
    TrophicIndex = as.numeric(TrophicIndex),
    TrophicGroup = as.character(TrophicGroup)
  ) %>%
  arrange(TrophicIndex)

write_csv(lake_retained_tbl_est, "LakeZurich_RetainedSpecies_TrophicIndex_TrophicGroup_EST.csv")

# Hypothesis-testing readiness: KW + Cliff's delta
predictors <- lake_g %>% select(where(is.numeric)) %>% names()
predictors <- setdiff(predictors, c("TrophicIndex"))

kw_tbl <- lapply(predictors, function(v) {
  dat <- lake_g %>% select(TrophicGroup, all_of(v)) %>% filter(!is.na(TrophicGroup), !is.na(.data[[v]]))
  kt <- kruskal.test(dat[[v]] ~ dat$TrophicGroup)
  tibble(Predictor = v, chi_sq = unname(kt$statistic), df = unname(kt$parameter), p_value = unname(kt$p.value))
}) %>% bind_rows() %>%
  mutate(p_adj_BH = p.adjust(p_value, method = "BH"))

cliffs_delta <- function(x, y) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]; y <- y[is.finite(y)]
  if (length(x) == 0 || length(y) == 0) return(NA_real_)
  diffs <- outer(x, y, "-")
  (sum(diffs > 0) - sum(diffs < 0)) / (length(x) * length(y))
}
cliff_mag <- function(d) {
  ad <- abs(d)
  dplyr::case_when(
    is.na(ad)  ~ NA_character_,
    ad < 0.147 ~ "negligible",
    ad < 0.33  ~ "small",
    ad < 0.474 ~ "medium",
    TRUE       ~ "large"
  )
}

cliff_tbl <- lapply(predictors, function(v) {
  dat <- lake_g %>% select(TrophicGroup, all_of(v)) %>%
    filter(!is.na(TrophicGroup), !is.na(.data[[v]]), TrophicGroup %in% c("Oligo_like","Copio_like"))
  x <- dat %>% filter(TrophicGroup == "Oligo_like") %>% pull(v)
  y <- dat %>% filter(TrophicGroup == "Copio_like") %>% pull(v)
  d <- cliffs_delta(x, y)
  tibble(Predictor = v, Cliff_delta = d, Magnitude = cliff_mag(d))
}) %>% bind_rows()

stats_summary_tbl_est <- kw_tbl %>%
  transmute(Predictor, KW_chi2 = chi_sq, KW_df = df, KW_p = p_value, KW_p_BH = p_adj_BH) %>%
  left_join(cliff_tbl, by = "Predictor") %>%
  mutate(
    Cliff_delta = round(as.numeric(Cliff_delta), 3),
    KW_p        = signif(as.numeric(KW_p), 3),
    KW_p_BH     = signif(as.numeric(KW_p_BH), 3)
  ) %>%
  arrange(KW_p_BH)

write_csv(stats_summary_tbl_est, "LakeZurich_TrophicIndex_PredictorStats_Summary_EST.csv")
# =============================================================================
```


## Block 10 — “Money figure” + hypothesis-testing readiness (technical + biological interpretation)

### What this figure establishes (and why it is reviewer-convincing)

This block demonstrates **three things at once**:

1. **Calibration (training-defined scale):** the trophic index is defined in the indicator (training) set, and Lake Zurich taxa are **projected into the same PC1 space** (Panels A–C).  
2. **Operational grouping for hypothesis testing:** fixed **training-anchored cutoffs** (Q25/Q75) define three *operational* strategy groups (**Oligo_like / Meso / Copio_like**) that can be used in downstream comparisons (Panels B–C).  
3. **Stability / fragility check:** the block quantifies how stable those cutoffs are under resampling of the small training set, and how many lake taxa sit close enough to the thresholds that their group assignment could plausibly flip under slightly different cutoffs (Panel D + “near-boundary” metric).

---

### Panel-by-panel interpretation

#### **A) Calibration + occupancy of index space (Training vs Lake densities)**
- The **lake distribution is shifted to lower index values** compared to the training set.
- This means Lake Zurich taxa occupy a **restricted, oligotroph-skewed subset** of the broader genomic strategy continuum spanned by the indicator species.
- Importantly, this is not a circular result: the **training** data define the axis and cutoffs; the **lake** data only reveal how environmental taxa populate that axis.

**Biological meaning:** the Lake Zurich MAG set is dominated by streamlined / low-investment genomic strategies, with relatively few taxa showing the high-index “copiotroph-like” trait combination.

#### **B) Training-anchored operational groups (Lake and Training shown separately)**
Cutoffs are fixed from the training set:
- **Q25 = −1.1469**, **Q75 = 1.3329**
- Training group sizes: **6 / 10 / 6** (as expected from quartiles in n=22)
- Lake group sizes: **102 / 150 / 16** (strong oligotroph bias)

**Interpretation:** using **training-anchored cutoffs** preserves *absolute meaning* of “extreme” strategies.  
If lake-specific quartiles were used, the “Copio_like” group would be redefined relative to an already oligotroph-dominated community, weakening comparability and biological interpretation.

#### **C) Ranked axis with training anchors**
- Grey points show **all lake taxa ranked along the same axis**.
- Colored/labeled training points serve as **anchors** that link the axis back to well-characterized indicator taxa.

**Interpretation:** this panel makes the index usable for readers: the axis has an ecological interpretation (streamlined → investment-rich) and the lake taxa can be interpreted relative to known reference organisms.

#### **D) Cutoff stability (bootstrap on training) + “near-boundary” lake fraction**
Because the training set is small (**n = 22**), quartile cutoffs are **order statistics** and can move noticeably under bootstrap resampling:
- Q25 bootstrap 95% CI: **[-2.262, -0.036]**
- Q75 bootstrap 95% CI: **[0.012, 1.975]**

This does **not** mean the index is unstable; it means the *exact numeric location* of Q25/Q75 has uncertainty when estimated from small n.

---

### What “lake near-boundary taxa” means (precise definition)

A lake taxon is counted as **near-boundary** if its trophic index lies within a small tolerance **ε** of **either cutoff** (Q25 or Q75):

\[
\text{near} =
\left|\text{TI} - Q25\right|\le \varepsilon
\quad \text{or} \quad
\left|\text{TI} - Q75\right|\le \varepsilon
\]

Here you defined:

\[
\varepsilon = 0.1 \times \mathrm{IQR}(\text{TI}_{train})
\]

In your run:
- **ε = 0.248**
- **52 / 268 lake taxa (19.4%)** are near a cutoff

**Interpretation:** ~19% of lake taxa sit close enough to the decision boundaries that small shifts in the training-estimated cutoffs (e.g., under bootstrap uncertainty) could change their discrete group assignment.  
This is a **fragility diagnostic**, not a failure:
- taxa far from cutoffs are **robustly classified**
- taxa near cutoffs are inherently “borderline” on a continuum (biologically reasonable)

**How to use this in hypothesis testing:**  
- For primary analyses, use all taxa with the fixed cutoffs (keeps power).  
- For sensitivity, rerun key tests after excluding near-boundary taxa (or treating TI continuously); robust conclusions should persist.

---

## Hypothesis-testing readiness: what the stats table is actually proving

Your summary table reports, per predictor, a **global Kruskal–Wallis test across the 3 lake groups**, plus an **effect size for extremes** (Cliff’s delta: Oligo_like vs Copio_like).

### Technical interpretation
- **Kruskal–Wallis** tests whether the distributions differ among the three groups (non-parametric; robust to skew and unequal n).
- **BH correction** controls false discovery rate across predictors.
- **Cliff’s delta** quantifies effect size between extremes:
  - δ ≈ −1: Copio_like almost always > Oligo_like
  - δ ≈ +1: Oligo_like almost always > Copio_like
  - magnitude labels summarize practical effect size.

### Biological interpretation (what differs between strategies)
The strongest signals are exactly the traits expected to define “investment vs streamlining”:

- **GENOME_LENGTH**: KW p ≪ 10⁻³⁰, **δ = −1 (large)**  
  → Copio_like genomes are consistently larger than Oligo_like genomes.

- **CODING_DENSITY**: KW p ≪ 10⁻³⁰, **δ = +0.962 (large)**  
  → Oligo_like genomes are consistently more streamlined/compact.

- **NUM_SIGMA**: KW p ≪ 10⁻²⁰, **δ = −1 (large)**  
  → Copio_like taxa have far higher regulatory capacity.

- **TOTAL_TRNA**: KW p ~ 10⁻⁸, **δ = −0.914 (large)**  
  → Copio_like taxa show higher translational investment.

- **MAX_RRNA**: KW p ~ 10⁻⁶, **δ = −0.472 (medium)**  
  → growth potential proxy separates extremes, but more weakly (expected for MAG-derived rRNA features and ecological decoupling).

Overall: the operational groups correspond to **biologically coherent and strongly separated genomic syndromes**, not arbitrary bins.

---

## Critical assessment (what a careful reviewer might ask, and how you answer)

1. **“Aren’t group differences inevitable because groups are defined on the same index?”**  
   Partly yes for traits used to compute the index, but your key defense is:
   - the cutoffs are **defined in training**, and the separation is shown in an **independent lake dataset**.
   - the result is a **generalization test**: the same axis meaningfully stratifies an environmental community.

2. **“Bootstrap CI of Q25/Q75 is wide.”**  
   True, because n=22 and quartiles are discrete. You explicitly quantify this and show its practical consequence (near-boundary fraction).  
   This strengthens credibility because you are not hiding uncertainty.

3. **“Does discretization contradict the continuum claim?”**  
   No—your grouping is explicitly **operational** for hypothesis tests.  
   The continuum is still preserved by:
   - reporting **continuous TI** in core figures (A/C)
   - using groups only as a pragmatic way to compare genomic/proteomic outcomes (B + downstream boxplots)
   - optionally including sensitivity analyses excluding near-boundary taxa.

---

## How to “sell” the take-home message (text you can reuse)

**“A training-defined genomic trophic index provides a calibrated, biologically anchored continuum of genomic investment strategies. When projected onto Lake Zurich taxa, the community occupies a predominantly oligotrophic region of this index space. Fixed training-anchored thresholds define operational strategy groups that enable hypothesis testing, and these groups show strong, coherent differences in genome size, streamlining, and regulatory/translational capacity. Bootstrap diagnostics quantify cutoff uncertainty given the small training set and identify a minority of near-boundary taxa whose discrete assignment is most sensitive—providing a transparent robustness check rather than an ad hoc categorization.”**


# publication plot
```{r}
# =============================================================================
# PUBLICATION FIGURE A (OBSERVED)
# Uses: dat_feat_ti, trophic_cols, lake_scored_obs
# Exports: Fig_TrophicIndex_Training_vs_LakeZurich_FINAL_OBS.pdf
# =============================================================================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(patchwork)
})

# -----------------------------
# 1) Training-anchored cutoffs (fixed reference)
# -----------------------------
q25_train <- unname(quantile(dat_feat_ti$TrophicIndex, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE))
q75_train <- unname(quantile(dat_feat_ti$TrophicIndex, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE))

group_cols <- c(
  Oligo_like   = "#2166AC",
  Intermediate = "grey60",
  Copio_like   = "#B2182B"
)

shade_layers <- function(q25, q75, group_cols) {
  list(
    annotate("rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, ymin = -Inf, ymax = q25,
             fill = group_cols["Oligo_like"], alpha = 0.06),
    annotate("rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, ymin = q25,  ymax = q75,
             fill = group_cols["Intermediate"], alpha = 0.05),
    annotate("rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, ymin = q75,  ymax = Inf,
             fill = group_cols["Copio_like"], alpha = 0.06),
    geom_hline(yintercept = c(q25, q75),
               linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.7, color = "grey35")
  )
}

# -----------------------------
# 2) TRAINING: ranked scatter + attached density
# -----------------------------
train_plot_df <- dat_feat_ti %>%
  mutate(
    TrophicGroup = case_when(
      TrophicIndex <= q25_train ~ "Oligo_like",
      TrophicIndex >= q75_train ~ "Copio_like",
      TRUE                      ~ "Intermediate"
    ),
    TrophicGroup = factor(TrophicGroup, levels = c("Oligo_like","Intermediate","Copio_like"))
  ) %>%
  arrange(TrophicIndex) %>%
  mutate(Rank = row_number())

train_y <- c(-3, 5)

p_train_rank <- ggplot(train_plot_df, aes(Rank, TrophicIndex)) +
  shade_layers(q25_train, q75_train, group_cols) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = Classification),
             shape = 21, color = "black", stroke = 0.35, size = 5, alpha = 0.75) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = trophic_cols) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 22), breaks = seq(0, 22, 4), expand = c(0.05, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = train_y, breaks = seq(-3, 5, 2), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = train_y, clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(5.5, 0, 5.5, 5.5)
  ) +
  labs(x = "Rank", y = "TrophicIndex", fill = NULL)

p_train_density <- ggplot(train_plot_df, aes(y = TrophicIndex)) +
  shade_layers(q25_train, q75_train, group_cols) +
  geom_density(aes(x = after_stat(density), fill = Classification, color = Classification),
               linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.35, adjust = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = trophic_cols) +
  scale_color_manual(values = trophic_cols) +
  geom_density(aes(x = after_stat(density)),
               color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1.1, adjust = 1) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = train_y, expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = train_y, clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text  = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(5.5, 5.5, 5.5, 0)
  )

train_rank_density_fig <- p_train_rank + p_train_density + plot_layout(widths = c(4, 2))

# -----------------------------
# 3) LAKE (OBSERVED): build grouped lake_g locally from lake_scored_obs
# -----------------------------
lake_plot_df <- lake_scored_obs %>%
  mutate(
    TrophicGroup = case_when(
      TrophicIndex <= q25_train ~ "Oligo_like",
      TrophicIndex >= q75_train ~ "Copio_like",
      TRUE                      ~ "Intermediate"
    ),
    TrophicGroup = factor(TrophicGroup, levels = c("Oligo_like","Intermediate","Copio_like")),
    Highlight = case_when(
      Phylum == "Planctomycetota" ~ "Planctomycetota",
      Genus  == "Planktophila"    ~ "Planktophila",
      Genus  == "Flavobacterium"  ~ "Flavobacterium",
      Genus  == "Fonsibacter"     ~ "Fonsibacter",
      TRUE                        ~ "Other"
    ),
    Highlight = factor(Highlight,
                       levels = c("Planctomycetota","Planktophila","Flavobacterium","Fonsibacter","Other"))
  ) %>%
  arrange(TrophicIndex) %>%
  mutate(Rank = row_number())

hl_cols <- c(
  Planctomycetota = "#7B3294",
  Planktophila    = "#008837",
  Flavobacterium  = "#E66101",
  Fonsibacter     = "#1F78B4",
  Other           = "grey75"
)

lake_y <- c(-3, 7)

p_lake_rank <- ggplot(lake_plot_df, aes(Rank, TrophicIndex)) +
  shade_layers(q25_train, q75_train, group_cols) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = Highlight),
             shape = 21, color = "black", stroke = 0.35, size = 5, alpha = 0.75) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = hl_cols,
                    breaks = c("Planctomycetota","Planktophila","Flavobacterium","Fonsibacter"),
                    drop = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, nrow(lake_plot_df)),
                     breaks = seq(0, nrow(lake_plot_df), 50),
                     expand = c(0.05, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = lake_y, breaks = seq(-3, 7, 2), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = lake_y, clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(5.5, 0, 5.5, 5.5)
  ) +
  labs(x = "Rank", y = "TrophicIndex", fill = NULL)

p_lake_density <- ggplot(lake_plot_df, aes(y = TrophicIndex)) +
  shade_layers(q25_train, q75_train, group_cols) +
  geom_density(aes(x = after_stat(density), fill = TrophicGroup, color = TrophicGroup),
               linewidth = 0.9, alpha = 0.35, adjust = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  geom_density(aes(x = after_stat(density)),
               color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1.1, adjust = 1) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = lake_y, expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = lake_y, clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text  = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(5.5, 5.5, 5.5, 0)
  )

lake_rank_density_fig <- p_lake_rank + p_lake_density + plot_layout(widths = c(4, 2))

# -----------------------------
# 4) FINAL stacked figure + export
# -----------------------------
final_train_lake_fig_obs <- train_rank_density_fig / lake_rank_density_fig +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 2))

final_train_lake_fig_obs

ggsave("Fig_TrophicIndex_Training_vs_LakeZurich_FINAL_OBS.pdf",
       final_train_lake_fig_obs, width = 12, height = 9, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
# =============================================================================
# PUBLICATION FIGURE B (ESTIMATED)
# Uses: dat_feat_ti, trophic_cols, lake_scored_est
# Exports: Fig_TrophicIndex_Training_vs_LakeZurich_FINAL_EST.pdf
# =============================================================================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(patchwork)
})

# -----------------------------
# 1) Training-anchored cutoffs (fixed reference)
# -----------------------------
q25_train <- unname(quantile(dat_feat_ti$TrophicIndex, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE))
q75_train <- unname(quantile(dat_feat_ti$TrophicIndex, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE))

# q25_train
# -1.146852
# q75_train
# 1.332919

group_cols <- c(
  Oligo_like   = "#2166AC",
  Intermediate = "grey60",
  Copio_like   = "#B2182B"
)

shade_layers <- function(q25, q75, group_cols) {
  list(
    annotate("rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, ymin = -Inf, ymax = q25,
             fill = group_cols["Oligo_like"], alpha = 0.06),
    annotate("rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, ymin = q25,  ymax = q75,
             fill = group_cols["Intermediate"], alpha = 0.05),
    annotate("rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, ymin = q75,  ymax = Inf,
             fill = group_cols["Copio_like"], alpha = 0.06),
    geom_hline(yintercept = c(q25, q75),
               linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.7, color = "grey35")
  )
}

# -----------------------------
# 2) TRAINING: ranked scatter + attached density (same as OBS)
# -----------------------------
train_plot_df <- dat_feat_ti %>%
  mutate(
    TrophicGroup = case_when(
      TrophicIndex <= q25_train ~ "Oligo_like",
      TrophicIndex >= q75_train ~ "Copio_like",
      TRUE                      ~ "Intermediate"
    ),
    TrophicGroup = factor(TrophicGroup, levels = c("Oligo_like","Intermediate","Copio_like"))
  ) %>%
  arrange(TrophicIndex) %>%
  mutate(Rank = row_number())

train_y <- c(-3, 5)

p_train_rank <- ggplot(train_plot_df, aes(Rank, TrophicIndex)) +
  shade_layers(q25_train, q75_train, group_cols) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = Classification),
             shape = 21, color = "black", stroke = 0.35, size = 5, alpha = 0.75) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = trophic_cols) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 22), breaks = seq(0, 22, 4), expand = c(0.05, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = train_y, breaks = seq(-3, 5, 2), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = train_y, clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(5.5, 0, 5.5, 5.5)
  ) +
  labs(x = "Rank", y = "TrophicIndex", fill = NULL)

p_train_density <- ggplot(train_plot_df, aes(y = TrophicIndex)) +
  shade_layers(q25_train, q75_train, group_cols) +
  geom_density(aes(x = after_stat(density), fill = Classification, color = Classification),
               linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.35, adjust = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = trophic_cols) +
  scale_color_manual(values = trophic_cols) +
  geom_density(aes(x = after_stat(density)),
               color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1.1, adjust = 1) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = train_y, expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = train_y, clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text  = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(5.5, 5.5, 5.5, 0)
  )

train_rank_density_fig <- p_train_rank + p_train_density + plot_layout(widths = c(4, 2))

# -----------------------------
# 3) LAKE (ESTIMATED): build grouped lake_g locally from lake_scored_est
# -----------------------------
lake_plot_df <- lake_scored_est %>%
  mutate(
    TrophicGroup = case_when(
      TrophicIndex <= q25_train ~ "Oligo_like",
      TrophicIndex >= q75_train ~ "Copio_like",
      TRUE                      ~ "Intermediate"
    ),
    TrophicGroup = factor(TrophicGroup, levels = c("Oligo_like","Intermediate","Copio_like")),
    Highlight = case_when(
      Phylum == "Planctomycetota" ~ "Planctomycetota",
      Genus  == "Planktophila"    ~ "Planktophila",
      Genus  == "Flavobacterium"  ~ "Flavobacterium",
      Genus  == "Fonsibacter"     ~ "Fonsibacter",
      TRUE                        ~ "Other"
    ),
    Highlight = factor(Highlight,
                       levels = c("Planctomycetota","Planktophila","Flavobacterium","Fonsibacter","Other"))
  ) %>%
  arrange(TrophicIndex) %>%
  mutate(Rank = row_number())

hl_cols <- c(
  Planctomycetota = "#7B3294",
  Planktophila    = "#008837",
  Flavobacterium  = "#E66101",
  Fonsibacter     = "#1F78B4",
  Other           = "grey75"
)

lake_y <- c(-3, 8)

p_lake_rank <- ggplot(lake_plot_df, aes(Rank, TrophicIndex)) +
  shade_layers(q25_train, q75_train, group_cols) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = Highlight),
             shape = 21, color = "black", stroke = 0.35, size = 5, alpha = 0.75) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = hl_cols,
                    breaks = c("Planctomycetota","Planktophila","Flavobacterium","Fonsibacter"),
                    drop = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, nrow(lake_plot_df)),
                     breaks = seq(0, nrow(lake_plot_df), 50),
                     expand = c(0.05, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = lake_y, breaks = seq(-3, 7, 2), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = lake_y, clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(5.5, 0, 5.5, 5.5)
  ) +
  labs(x = "Rank", y = "TrophicIndex", fill = NULL)

p_lake_density <- ggplot(lake_plot_df, aes(y = TrophicIndex)) +
  shade_layers(q25_train, q75_train, group_cols) +
  geom_density(aes(x = after_stat(density), fill = TrophicGroup, color = TrophicGroup),
               linewidth = 0.9, alpha = 0.35, adjust = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  geom_density(aes(x = after_stat(density)),
               color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1.1, adjust = 1) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = lake_y, expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = lake_y, clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text  = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(5.5, 5.5, 5.5, 0)
  )

lake_rank_density_fig <- p_lake_rank + p_lake_density + plot_layout(widths = c(4, 2))

# -----------------------------
# 4) FINAL stacked figure + export
# -----------------------------
final_train_lake_fig_est <- train_rank_density_fig / lake_rank_density_fig +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 2))

final_train_lake_fig_est

ggsave("Fig_TrophicIndex_Training_vs_LakeZurich_FINAL_EST.pdf",
       final_train_lake_fig_est, width = 11, height = 14.5, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```

## Reviewer-facing methodological summary and interpretation

### Methodological goal
- Derive a **continuous genomic trophic strategy axis** from indicator species with known lifestyles.
- Use this axis as a **reference strategy space** onto which lake community genomes can be projected.
- Quantify **ecosystem occupancy of trophic strategies** without re-fitting or re-scaling the axis.

---

### What is done (training / indicator species)
- A genomic feature matrix from indicator species is reduced to a single **TrophicIndex** (PC1).
- Species are ranked along this axis to visualize monotonic variation in trophic strategy.
- **A priori trophic labels** (oligotroph, mesotroph, copiotroph) are *not used to define the axis*, but are used for **post hoc validation**.
- Training quartiles (Q25/Q75) of the TrophicIndex define **operational regions**:
  - Oligo-like (≤ Q25)
  - Intermediate (Q25–Q75)
  - Copio-like (≥ Q75)
- Side densities show:
  - Colored densities: \( p(\text{TrophicIndex} \mid \text{known trophic class}) \)
  - Black outline: overall \( p(\text{TrophicIndex}) \)
- Purpose:
  - Validate that the learned axis **recovers known trophic strategies**.
  - Demonstrate overlap and continuity rather than discrete class separation.

---

### What is done (Lake Zurich projection)
- Lake genomes are **projected onto the fixed training-defined TrophicIndex**.
- No re-training, re-centering, or lake-specific thresholds are applied.
- Species are ranked to visualize **relative positioning within the reference strategy space**.
- Training Q25/Q75 cutoffs are reused to define **operational trophic groups** in the lake.
- Side densities show:
  - Colored densities: \( p(\text{TrophicIndex} \mid \text{operational region}) \)
  - Black outline: overall lake \( p(\text{TrophicIndex}) \)
- Purpose:
  - Quantify how the lake community **occupies** the reference trophic space.
  - Identify enrichment or depletion of oligotrophic vs copiotrophic strategies.

---

### Why densities are shown differently in training vs lake
- **Training densities** are conditioned on *external labels*:
  - Used for **biological validation** of the learned axis.
  - Avoids circularity (index is not used to define its own groups).
- **Lake densities** are conditioned on *index-derived operational regions*:
  - No external trophic labels exist.
  - Used for **ecological interpretation and comparison**.
- This asymmetry is intentional and reflects two distinct scientific questions:
  - *Does the axis capture known biology?* (training)
  - *How does the ecosystem occupy that axis?* (lake)

---

### Why the overall density is drawn on top
- The black density line represents the **marginal distribution** of the data.
- Drawing it last improves visibility without altering underlying data.
- Group densities remain semi-transparent and interpretable.
- This follows standard practice in trait-based ecology and latent-axis visualization.

---

### Key interpretation
- The TrophicIndex captures a **continuous gradient** rather than discrete trophic classes.
- Known oligotrophs and copiotrophs are shifted toward expected ends of the axis but overlap.
- Lake Zurich communities show **non-uniform occupancy** of the reference trophic space.
- Extremes of the axis are disproportionately occupied by specific operational strategies.
- This framework enables **direct, quantitative comparison** of trophic organization across systems using a fixed genomic reference axis.

---

### Conceptual takeaway
> The training set defines a biologically interpretable trophic strategy space;  
> the lake community is projected into this space to reveal how ecological structure emerges along a continuous genomic gradient.

```{r}
# =============================================================================
# FIGURE A — FINAL MODEL-JUSTIFICATION (OBSERVED lake traits)
# Produces: Fig_ModelJustification_BoxplotsPlusVariance_FINAL_OBS.pdf
#
# Uses (already loaded):
#   dat_feat      (training; must contain Species_ID, Classification, and pca_vars)
#   trophic_cols  (named colors for Classification)
#   pca_vars      (curated trait names; here we map to OBS lake columns)
#   lake_dat      (lake table with Obs_* and/or GENOME_LENGTH, CODING_DENSITY)
#
# Notes:
#   - PCA + permutation test are done on TRAINING only (as in your original code).
#   - Panel A shows TRAINING boxplots/points (z-scored) + PC1 loadings/correlations.
#   - This OBS block additionally computes lake TrophicIndex (observed traits) and
#     overlays Lake density as a grey line in Panel BOTTOM (simple + interpretable).
# =============================================================================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(tibble)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(patchwork)
})

set.seed(123)
n_perm <- 5000

# -----------------------------
# 0) Map curated traits to OBSERVED lake columns
# -----------------------------
# Training uses: GENOME_LENGTH, CODING_DENSITY, TOTAL_TRNA, NUM_SIGMA
# Lake OBS uses: GENOME_LENGTH, CODING_DENSITY, Obs_TOTAL_TRNA, Obs_NUM_SIGMA
lake_trait_map_obs <- c(
  GENOME_LENGTH  = "GENOME_LENGTH",
  CODING_DENSITY = "CODING_DENSITY",
  TOTAL_TRNA     = "Obs_TOTAL_TRNA",
  NUM_SIGMA      = "Obs_NUM_SIGMA"
)

# -----------------------------
# 1) TRAINING PCA on curated traits (z-scored)
# -----------------------------
pca_df <- dat_feat %>%
  select(Species_ID, Classification, all_of(pca_vars)) %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(pca_vars), as.numeric)) %>%
  drop_na()

X <- scale(as.matrix(pca_df[, pca_vars]), center = TRUE, scale = TRUE)

pca_res <- prcomp(X, center = FALSE, scale. = FALSE)
eig_vals <- pca_res$sdev^2
var_exp  <- eig_vals / sum(eig_vals)

scores <- as.data.frame(pca_res$x) %>%
  mutate(
    Species_ID = pca_df$Species_ID,
    Classification = pca_df$Classification
  )

loadings <- as.data.frame(pca_res$rotation) %>%
  rownames_to_column("Trait")

# Orient PC1 so GENOME_LENGTH loads positively
if (loadings$PC1[loadings$Trait == "GENOME_LENGTH"] < 0) {
  loadings$PC1 <- -loadings$PC1
  scores$PC1   <- -scores$PC1
}

# Store training center/scale + PC1 weights for lake projection
train_center <- colMeans(pca_df[, pca_vars])
train_scale  <- apply(pca_df[, pca_vars], 2, sd)
w_pc1 <- loadings$PC1
names(w_pc1) <- loadings$Trait

# -----------------------------
# 2) Permutation null (variance explained; training only)
# -----------------------------
perm_var <- replicate(n_perm, {
  Xp <- apply(X, 2, sample)
  prcomp(scale(Xp), center = FALSE, scale. = FALSE)$sdev^2
})
perm_var <- t(perm_var / colSums(perm_var))

pc1_p <- (1 + sum(perm_var[, 1] >= var_exp[1])) / (1 + n_perm)

null_summary <- tibble(
  PC = seq_along(var_exp),
  obs = var_exp,
  null_mean = colMeans(perm_var),
  null_lo = apply(perm_var, 2, quantile, 0.025),
  null_hi = apply(perm_var, 2, quantile, 0.975)
)

# -----------------------------
# 3) Trait z-scores + correlations with PC1 (training)
# -----------------------------
trait_long <- pca_df %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(pca_vars), scale)) %>%
  bind_cols(scores %>% select(PC1)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(pca_vars), names_to = "Trait", values_to = "Trait_z") %>%
  mutate(
    Trait = factor(Trait, levels = pca_vars),
    Classification = factor(Classification, levels = names(trophic_cols))
  )

trait_corr <- trait_long %>%
  group_by(Trait) %>%
  summarise(r = cor(Trait_z, PC1), .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(loadings, by = c("Trait" = "Trait")) %>%
  mutate(label = paste0("PC1=", sprintf("%.2f", PC1), " | r=", sprintf("%.2f", r)))

y_annot <- max(trait_long$Trait_z, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.55

# -----------------------------
# 4) Panel A — Training boxplots + points + loading/correlation labels
# -----------------------------
pA <- ggplot(trait_long, aes(Trait, Trait_z, fill = Classification)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_point(
    position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.3, dodge.width = 0.75),
    shape = 21, size = 3, color = "black", stroke = 0.2, alpha = 0.65
  ) +
  geom_boxplot(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.75),
    width = 0.55, outlier.shape = NA, color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.35, alpha = 0.30
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = trait_corr,
    aes(Trait, y_annot, label = label),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 3.1
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = trophic_cols) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "top", panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) +
  labs(
    title = "Standardized genomic trait distributions defining the trophic index (TRAINING)",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "PC1=", round(var_exp[1] * 100, 1), "%; PC2=", round(var_exp[2] * 100, 1),
      "% | Permutation p(PC1)=", signif(pc1_p, 3),
      " (n_perm=", n_perm, ") | Lake projection shown below: OBS"
    ),
    x = NULL, y = "Trait value (z-score)", fill = NULL
  )

# -----------------------------
# 5) Lake projection (OBS) — compute TrophicIndex using training params
# -----------------------------
lake_obs <- lake_dat %>%
  transmute(
    Species_ID = Species_ID,
    GENOME_LENGTH  = as.numeric(.data[[lake_trait_map_obs["GENOME_LENGTH"]]]),
    CODING_DENSITY = as.numeric(.data[[lake_trait_map_obs["CODING_DENSITY"]]]),
    TOTAL_TRNA     = as.numeric(.data[[lake_trait_map_obs["TOTAL_TRNA"]]]),
    NUM_SIGMA      = as.numeric(.data[[lake_trait_map_obs["NUM_SIGMA"]]])
  ) %>%
  filter(complete.cases(.))

Z_lake <- sweep(as.matrix(lake_obs[, pca_vars]), 2, train_center, "-")
Z_lake <- sweep(Z_lake, 2, train_scale, "/")
lake_obs$TrophicIndex <- as.numeric(Z_lake %*% w_pc1)

train_ti <- scores$PC1

# -----------------------------
# 6) Panel B — Variance explained + simple index density overlay (Training vs Lake OBS)
# -----------------------------
pB <- ggplot(null_summary, aes(PC)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = null_lo, ymax = null_hi), width = 0.18) +
  geom_line(aes(y = null_mean), linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(aes(y = null_mean)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = obs), color = "red", linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(aes(y = obs), color = "red", size = 2.6) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq_along(var_exp)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.8), breaks = seq(0, 0.8, by = 0.2)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  labs(
    title = "Variance explained by PCA (TRAINING; observed vs permutation null)",
    subtitle = "Bottom overlay: distribution of PC1 (training) vs projected lake index (OBS)",
    x = "Principal component",
    y = "Proportion variance explained"
  )

# small attached density panel (kept simple)
pB_den <- ggplot() +
  geom_density(aes(x = train_ti), color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_density(aes(x = lake_obs$TrophicIndex), color = "grey50", linewidth = 1) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(x = "PC1 / TrophicIndex", y = NULL)

fig_model_justify_obs <- pA / (pB / pB_den + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 0.65))) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1.35, 1.0))

fig_model_justify_obs

ggsave("Fig_ModelJustification_BoxplotsPlusVariance_FINAL_OBS.pdf",
       fig_model_justify_obs, width = 9, height = 9, units = "in", device = pdf)
```
```{r}
# =============================================================================
# FIGURE B — FINAL MODEL-JUSTIFICATION (ESTIMATED lake traits)
# Produces: Fig_ModelJustification_BoxplotsPlusVariance_FINAL_EST.pdf
#
# Same as OBS block, but lake projection uses:
#   Est_TOTAL_TRNA and Est_NUM_SIGMA
# =============================================================================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(tibble)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(patchwork)
})

set.seed(123)
n_perm <- 5000

# -----------------------------
# 0) Map curated traits to ESTIMATED lake columns
# -----------------------------
lake_trait_map_est <- c(
  GENOME_LENGTH  = "GENOME_LENGTH",
  CODING_DENSITY = "CODING_DENSITY",
  TOTAL_TRNA     = "Est_TOTAL_TRNA",
  NUM_SIGMA      = "Est_NUM_SIGMA"
)

# -----------------------------
# 1) TRAINING PCA on curated traits (z-scored)
# -----------------------------
pca_df <- dat_feat %>%
  select(Species_ID, Classification, all_of(pca_vars)) %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(pca_vars), as.numeric)) %>%
  drop_na()

X <- scale(as.matrix(pca_df[, pca_vars]), center = TRUE, scale = TRUE)

pca_res <- prcomp(X, center = FALSE, scale. = FALSE)
eig_vals <- pca_res$sdev^2
var_exp  <- eig_vals / sum(eig_vals)

scores <- as.data.frame(pca_res$x) %>%
  mutate(
    Species_ID = pca_df$Species_ID,
    Classification = pca_df$Classification
  )

loadings <- as.data.frame(pca_res$rotation) %>%
  rownames_to_column("Trait")

# Orient PC1 so GENOME_LENGTH loads positively
if (loadings$PC1[loadings$Trait == "GENOME_LENGTH"] < 0) {
  loadings$PC1 <- -loadings$PC1
  scores$PC1   <- -scores$PC1
}

train_center <- colMeans(pca_df[, pca_vars])
train_scale  <- apply(pca_df[, pca_vars], 2, sd)
w_pc1 <- loadings$PC1
names(w_pc1) <- loadings$Trait

# -----------------------------
# 2) Permutation null (variance explained; training only)
# -----------------------------
perm_var <- replicate(n_perm, {
  Xp <- apply(X, 2, sample)
  prcomp(scale(Xp), center = FALSE, scale. = FALSE)$sdev^2
})
perm_var <- t(perm_var / colSums(perm_var))

pc1_p <- (1 + sum(perm_var[, 1] >= var_exp[1])) / (1 + n_perm)

null_summary <- tibble(
  PC = seq_along(var_exp),
  obs = var_exp,
  null_mean = colMeans(perm_var),
  null_lo = apply(perm_var, 2, quantile, 0.025),
  null_hi = apply(perm_var, 2, quantile, 0.975)
)

# -----------------------------
# 3) Trait z-scores + correlations with PC1 (training)
# -----------------------------
trait_long <- pca_df %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(pca_vars), scale)) %>%
  bind_cols(scores %>% select(PC1)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(pca_vars), names_to = "Trait", values_to = "Trait_z") %>%
  mutate(
    Trait = factor(Trait, levels = pca_vars),
    Classification = factor(Classification, levels = names(trophic_cols))
  )

trait_corr <- trait_long %>%
  group_by(Trait) %>%
  summarise(r = cor(Trait_z, PC1), .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(loadings, by = c("Trait" = "Trait")) %>%
  mutate(label = paste0("PC1=", sprintf("%.2f", PC1), " | r=", sprintf("%.2f", r)))

y_annot <- max(trait_long$Trait_z, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.55

# -----------------------------
# 4) Panel A — Training boxplots + points + loading/correlation labels
# -----------------------------
pA <- ggplot(trait_long, aes(Trait, Trait_z, fill = Classification)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_point(
    position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.3, dodge.width = 0.75),
    shape = 21, size = 3, color = "black", stroke = 0.2, alpha = 0.65
  ) +
  geom_boxplot(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.75),
    width = 0.55, outlier.shape = NA, color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.35, alpha = 0.30
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = trait_corr,
    aes(Trait, y_annot, label = label),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 3.1
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = trophic_cols) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "top", panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) +
  labs(
    title = "Standardized genomic trait distributions defining the trophic index (TRAINING)",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "PC1=", round(var_exp[1] * 100, 1), "%; PC2=", round(var_exp[2] * 100, 1),
      "% | Permutation p(PC1)=", signif(pc1_p, 3),
      " (n_perm=", n_perm, ") | Lake projection shown below: EST"
    ),
    x = NULL, y = "Trait value (z-score)", fill = NULL
  )

# -----------------------------
# 5) Lake projection (EST) — compute TrophicIndex using training params
# -----------------------------
lake_est <- lake_dat %>%
  transmute(
    Species_ID = Species_ID,
    GENOME_LENGTH  = as.numeric(.data[[lake_trait_map_est["GENOME_LENGTH"]]]),
    CODING_DENSITY = as.numeric(.data[[lake_trait_map_est["CODING_DENSITY"]]]),
    TOTAL_TRNA     = as.numeric(.data[[lake_trait_map_est["TOTAL_TRNA"]]]),
    NUM_SIGMA      = as.numeric(.data[[lake_trait_map_est["NUM_SIGMA"]]])
  ) %>%
  filter(complete.cases(.))

Z_lake <- sweep(as.matrix(lake_est[, pca_vars]), 2, train_center, "-")
Z_lake <- sweep(Z_lake, 2, train_scale, "/")
lake_est$TrophicIndex <- as.numeric(Z_lake %*% w_pc1)

train_ti <- scores$PC1

# -----------------------------
# 6) Panel B — Variance explained + simple index density overlay (Training vs Lake EST)
# -----------------------------
pB <- ggplot(null_summary, aes(PC)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = null_lo, ymax = null_hi), width = 0.18) +
  geom_line(aes(y = null_mean), linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(aes(y = null_mean)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = obs), color = "red", linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(aes(y = obs), color = "red", size = 2.6) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq_along(var_exp)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.8), breaks = seq(0, 0.8, by = 0.2)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  labs(
    title = "Variance explained by PCA (TRAINING; observed vs permutation null)",
    subtitle = "Bottom overlay: distribution of PC1 (training) vs projected lake index (EST)",
    x = "Principal component",
    y = "Proportion variance explained"
  )

pB_den <- ggplot() +
  geom_density(aes(x = train_ti), color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_density(aes(x = lake_est$TrophicIndex), color = "grey50", linewidth = 1) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(x = "PC1 / TrophicIndex", y = NULL)

fig_model_justify_est <- pA / (pB / pB_den + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 0.65))) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1.35, 1.0))

fig_model_justify_est

ggsave("Fig_ModelJustification_BoxplotsPlusVariance_FINAL_EST.pdf",
       fig_model_justify_est, width = 9, height = 9, units = "in", device = pdf)
```


### Genomic trophic index: construction, validation, and biological interpretation

- **What was done**
  - We quantified four curated genomic traits capturing complementary aspects of microbial life-history strategy: genome size (GENOME_LENGTH), genome streamlining (CODING_DENSITY), translational investment (TOTAL_TRNA), and regulatory flexibility (NUM_SIGMA).
  - Traits were **z-standardized across indicator species** and integrated using principal component analysis (PCA).
  - The first principal component (PC1) was oriented such that increasing values correspond to increasing genome size and metabolic breadth, and is hereafter referred to as the **genomic trophic index**.
  - The statistical significance of PC1 dominance was evaluated using a **permutation-based null model** that destroys cross-trait covariance while preserving marginal trait distributions.

- **Why this approach**
  - Individual genomic traits are correlated but noisy proxies of trophic strategy; no single trait is expected to fully capture trophic life-history variation.
  - PCA provides a data-driven way to identify a **latent axis of coordinated genomic investment**, while permutation testing distinguishes true biological structure from random covariance.
  - Trait standardization ensures that all traits contribute comparably and that the resulting index reflects **relative investment patterns**, not absolute trait magnitudes.

- **Key results**
  - PC1 explains **75.9% of total variance**, far exceeding expectations under the permutation null model (**PC1 perm. p < 0.001; n_perm = 5000**), while PC2 explains only 12.8%.
  - This steep drop in variance indicates that genomic trait variation among indicator species is effectively **one-dimensional**.
  - All curated traits show **strong monotonic correlations with PC1** (|r| ≈ 0.78–0.96), and similar absolute PC1 loadings (≈0.45–0.55), indicating that they contribute comparably to the index.
  - The direction of correlations is biologically coherent: genome length, tRNA copy number, and sigma factor diversity increase with PC1, whereas coding density decreases.

- **Biological interpretation**
  - PC1 captures a continuum from **streamlined oligotrophic genomes** (small, compact genomes with limited regulatory and translational investment) to **copiotrophic genomes** (larger, less streamlined genomes with greater regulatory and translational capacity).
  - The similarity of PC1 loadings across traits indicates **coordinated genomic investment**, rather than dominance by any single variable.
  - Traits that do not show sharp separation between a priori trophic classes still contribute meaningfully by reinforcing the shared life-history axis.

- **What the reported values mean**
  - **PC1 variance explained (75.9%)**: the proportion of total standardized trait variance captured by the trophic index.
  - **Permutation p-value (<0.001)**: the probability that such a dominant axis arises by chance in uncorrelated trait data.
  - **Trait–PC1 correlations (r)**: the strength and direction with which each trait aligns with the trophic index.

- **What these results do not imply**
  - The index is **not** equivalent to genome length alone, despite strong correlation; it integrates multiple genomic dimensions and is therefore more robust to noise and trait-specific biases.
  - Non-discrete separation of trophic classes does **not** indicate weak signal; rather, it supports the interpretation of trophic strategy as a **continuous spectrum**.
  - PC1 is not a classifier but a **quantitative life-history gradient** suitable for downstream projection and comparative analyses.

- **Conclusion**
  - Together, these results justify PC1 as a statistically robust and biologically interpretable **genomic trophic index**, capturing coordinated life-history variation across microbial genomes and enabling consistent comparison between reference species and environmental genomic datasets.


# Find what has changed in the trophic index and trophic groups because of completion of the mags
```{r}
# =============================================================================
# Compare OBSERVED vs ESTIMATED trophic index (Lake Zurich)
# + Identify taxa that change trophic group and visualize associated trait shifts
#
# Produces:
#   A) Density comparison (Observed vs Estimated) + training Q25/Q75 cutoffs
#   B) Rank-profile comparison (Observed-ranked) + training cutoffs
#   C) Group agreement tables (counts + row-wise % within OBS group)
#   D) Change figure:
#        - Delta TI vs Completeness (red = changed group)
#        - Dumbbell TI_obs -> TI_est for changed taxa + cutoff lines
#   E) Trait overview (one-row panels; dumbbells for changed taxa):
#        Genome size, Total tRNA, Sigma factors, Coding density
#
# REQUIRED objects already in your session:
#   - lake_scored_obs : tibble/data.frame with Species_ID, TrophicIndex (OBS)
#   - lake_scored_est : tibble/data.frame with Species_ID, TrophicIndex (EST)
#   - train_scored    : tibble/data.frame with TrophicIndex (to define cutoffs)
#   - lake_dat        : tibble/data.frame with Species_ID and trait columns listed below
#       * Completeness
#       * GENOME_LENGTH, Estimated_Genome_Size
#       * Obs_TOTAL_TRNA, Est_TOTAL_TRNA
#       * Obs_NUM_SIGMA, Est_NUM_SIGMA
#       * CODING_DENSITY
#
# OUTPUT FILE NAMING:
#   All exports are prefixed with: "ChangedTaxa_"
# =============================================================================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(patchwork)
  library(readr)
  library(scales)
})

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 0) Helpers
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Training-anchored trophic cutoffs (shared across OBS + EST)
q25_train <- unname(quantile(train_scored$TrophicIndex, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE))
q75_train <- unname(quantile(train_scored$TrophicIndex, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE))

assign_group <- function(x, q25 = q25_train, q75 = q75_train) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    x <= q25 ~ "Oligo_like",
    x >= q75 ~ "Copio_like",
    TRUE     ~ "Intermediate"
  )
}

# Base file prefix for ALL exports
out_prefix <- "ChangedTaxa_"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Build paired comparison table (intersection only)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

cmp <- inner_join(
  lake_scored_obs %>% transmute(Species_ID, TI_obs = as.numeric(TrophicIndex)),
  lake_scored_est %>% transmute(Species_ID, TI_est = as.numeric(TrophicIndex)),
  by = "Species_ID"
) %>%
  left_join(
    lake_dat %>% select(Species_ID, Completeness),
    by = "Species_ID"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Group_obs = factor(assign_group(TI_obs),
                       levels = c("Oligo_like", "Intermediate", "Copio_like")),
    Group_est = factor(assign_group(TI_est),
                       levels = c("Oligo_like", "Intermediate", "Copio_like")),
    Delta     = TI_est - TI_obs,
    Changed   = Group_obs != Group_est
  )

cat("\n[OBS vs EST] Paired taxa (intersection):", nrow(cmp), "\n")
cat("[OBS vs EST] Taxa that changed group:", sum(cmp$Changed), "\n\n")

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Plot A — Distribution comparison (density)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

den_df <- cmp %>%
  select(TI_obs, TI_est) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Type", values_to = "TrophicIndex") %>%
  mutate(Type = recode(Type, TI_obs = "Observed", TI_est = "Estimated"))

p_den <- ggplot(den_df, aes(TrophicIndex, color = Type)) +
  geom_density(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(q25_train, q75_train),
             linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8, color = "grey35") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(Observed = "black", Estimated = "grey50")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  labs(
    title = "Lake Zurich trophic index distribution: Observed vs Estimated",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Dashed = training Q25/Q75 (Q25=", round(q25_train, 2),
      ", Q75=", round(q75_train, 2), ")"
    ),
    x = "TrophicIndex (training PC1 score)",
    y = "Density",
    color = NULL
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

print(p_den)

ggsave(
  filename = paste0(out_prefix, "TrophicIndex_Distribution_OBS_vs_EST.pdf"),
  plot = p_den,
  width = 7.5, height = 4.5, units = "in", device = pdf
)

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Plot B — Rank-profile comparison (rank by OBS; show cutoffs as hlines)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

rank_df <- cmp %>%
  arrange(TI_obs) %>%
  mutate(Rank = row_number()) %>%
  select(Species_ID, Rank, TI_obs, TI_est) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(TI_obs, TI_est), names_to = "Type", values_to = "TrophicIndex") %>%
  mutate(Type = recode(Type, TI_obs = "Observed", TI_est = "Estimated"))

p_rank <- ggplot(rank_df, aes(Rank, TrophicIndex, color = Type)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(q25_train, q75_train),
             linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8, color = "grey35") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9, alpha = 0.95) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(Observed = "black", Estimated = "grey50")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  labs(
    title = "Lake Zurich trophic index rank profile: Observed vs Estimated",
    subtitle = "Rank order defined by Observed index (direct comparison of profiles)",
    x = "Lake taxa rank (sorted by Observed TrophicIndex)",
    y = "TrophicIndex (training PC1 score)",
    color = NULL
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

print(p_rank)

ggsave(
  filename = paste0(out_prefix, "TrophicIndex_Rank_OBS_vs_EST.pdf"),
  plot = p_rank,
  width = 8.5, height = 4.8, units = "in", device = pdf
)

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Tables — Group agreement (counts + row-wise % within OBS group)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

tab_counts <- cmp %>%
  count(Group_obs, Group_est) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = Group_est, values_from = n, values_fill = 0)

tab_props <- cmp %>%
  count(Group_obs, Group_est) %>%
  group_by(Group_obs) %>%
  mutate(pct_within_obs = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1)) %>%
  ungroup()

cat("[OBS vs EST] Group agreement (counts):\n")
print(tab_counts)

cat("\n[OBS vs EST] Group agreement (row-wise % within OBS group):\n")
print(tab_props)

write_csv(tab_counts, paste0(out_prefix, "TrophicGroup_ObsVsEst_Counts.csv"))
write_csv(tab_props,  paste0(out_prefix, "TrophicGroup_ObsVsEst_RowPercent.csv"))

shift_summary <- cmp %>%
  summarise(
    mean_shift       = mean(Delta, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_shift        = max(Delta,  na.rm = TRUE),
    n_changed_group  = sum(Changed, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

cat("\n[OBS vs EST] Shift summary:\n")
print(shift_summary)

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) Plot C — Changed taxa focus figure:
#    Panel A: Delta vs Completeness (red = changed group)
#    Panel B: Dumbbell TI_obs -> TI_est for changed taxa + vertical cutoff lines
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Panel A: Delta vs completeness
p_delta <- ggplot(cmp, aes(Completeness, Delta, color = Changed)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(size = 2.8, alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(`FALSE` = "grey70", `TRUE` = "#B2182B")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  labs(
    title = "Shift in trophic index after completion correction",
    subtitle = "Red = taxa that changed operational trophic group",
    x = "MAG Completeness (%)",
    y = "Δ TrophicIndex (Estimated − Observed)",
    color = "Group changed"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

# Panel B: Dumbbell for changed-only taxa
changed_only <- cmp %>%
  filter(Changed) %>%
  arrange(TI_obs) %>%
  mutate(Species_ID = factor(Species_ID, levels = Species_ID))

p_dumbbell_ti <- ggplot(changed_only) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(q25_train, q75_train),
             linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8, color = "grey40") +
  geom_segment(aes(x = TI_obs, xend = TI_est, y = Species_ID, yend = Species_ID),
               linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(x = TI_obs, y = Species_ID), color = "black", size = 3) +
  geom_point(aes(x = TI_est, y = Species_ID), color = "#B2182B", size = 3) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Taxa that changed trophic group",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Black = Observed | Red = Estimated | Training cutoffs: Q25=", round(q25_train, 2),
      ", Q75=", round(q75_train, 2)
    ),
    x = "TrophicIndex (training PC1 score)",
    y = NULL
  )

final_change_fig <- p_delta / p_dumbbell_ti +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1))

print(final_change_fig)

ggsave(
  filename = paste0(out_prefix, "TrophicGroup_Changes_OBS_vs_EST_withCutoffs.pdf"),
  plot = final_change_fig,
  width = 9, height = 9, units = "in", device = pdf
)

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6) Plot D — Trait overview for changed taxa (one row of dumbbell panels)
#    - Species ordered by observed TI (same ordering as above)
#    - Y labels only on first panel
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

changed_ids <- changed_only %>%
  pull(Species_ID) %>%
  as.character() %>%
  unique()

# Species order = observed TI order (stable + matches earlier plots)
sp_order <- cmp %>%
  filter(Species_ID %in% changed_ids) %>%
  arrange(TI_obs) %>%
  pull(Species_ID)

trait_cmp <- lake_dat %>%
  filter(Species_ID %in% changed_ids) %>%
  transmute(
    Species_ID,

    Genome_obs = as.numeric(GENOME_LENGTH),
    Genome_est = as.numeric(Estimated_Genome_Size),

    tRNA_obs   = as.numeric(Obs_TOTAL_TRNA),
    tRNA_est   = as.numeric(Est_TOTAL_TRNA),

    Sigma_obs  = as.numeric(Obs_NUM_SIGMA),
    Sigma_est  = as.numeric(Est_NUM_SIGMA),

    CD_obs     = as.numeric(CODING_DENSITY),
    CD_est     = as.numeric(CODING_DENSITY)   # unchanged; explicit for dumbbell
  ) %>%
  mutate(Species_ID = factor(Species_ID, levels = sp_order))

trait_long <- bind_rows(
  trait_cmp %>% transmute(Species_ID, Trait = "Genome size (bp)",        Obs = Genome_obs, Est = Genome_est),
  trait_cmp %>% transmute(Species_ID, Trait = "Total tRNA (count)",      Obs = tRNA_obs,   Est = tRNA_est),
  trait_cmp %>% transmute(Species_ID, Trait = "Sigma factors (count)",   Obs = Sigma_obs,  Est = Sigma_est),
  trait_cmp %>% transmute(Species_ID, Trait = "Coding density (%)",      Obs = CD_obs,     Est = CD_est)
) %>%
  mutate(Trait = factor(Trait, levels = c(
    "Genome size (bp)",
    "Total tRNA (count)",
    "Sigma factors (count)",
    "Coding density (%)"
  )))

make_trait_panel <- function(df, show_y = FALSE) {
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(y = Species_ID)) +
    geom_segment(aes(x = Obs, xend = Est, yend = Species_ID), linewidth = 0.9) +
    geom_point(aes(x = Obs), color = "black", size = 2.4) +
    geom_point(aes(x = Est), color = "#B2182B", size = 2.4) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.title.y = element_blank(),
      axis.title.x = element_blank()
    )

  if (!show_y) {
    p <- p + theme(
      axis.text.y  = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.y = element_blank()
    )
  } else {
    p <- p + theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9))
  }

  p
}

p1 <- make_trait_panel(trait_long %>% filter(Trait == "Genome size (bp)"), show_y = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Genome size") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma)

p2 <- make_trait_panel(trait_long %>% filter(Trait == "Total tRNA (count)"), show_y = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Total tRNA")

p3 <- make_trait_panel(trait_long %>% filter(Trait == "Sigma factors (count)"), show_y = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Sigma factors")

p4 <- make_trait_panel(trait_long %>% filter(Trait == "Coding density (%)"), show_y = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Coding density")

final_trait_row <- (p1 | p2 | p3 | p4) +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1.35, 1, 1, 1)) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Observed vs completion-corrected traits for taxa that changed trophic group",
    subtitle = "Black = observed | Red = estimated (completion-corrected). Species ordered by observed TrophicIndex."
  )

print(final_trait_row)

ggsave(
  filename = paste0(out_prefix, "ObsVsEst_Traits_ChangedTaxa_OneRow.pdf"),
  plot = final_trait_row,
  width = 14,
  height = 0.22 * length(sp_order) + 2.3,  # scales with # taxa
  units = "in",
  device = pdf
)

# =============================================================================
# Minimal export — lake_dat rows for taxa that changed trophic group,
# plus OBS/EST trophic index and OBS/EST trophic group
#
# Requires in memory:
#   - cmp (must contain Species_ID, TI_obs, TI_est, Group_obs, Group_est, Changed)
#   - lake_dat (taxonomy + traits; contains Species_ID)
#
# Output:
#   - ChangedTaxa_LakeDat_WithObsEstTIandGroups.csv
# =============================================================================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(readr)
})

changed_ids <- cmp %>%
  filter(Changed) %>%
  pull(Species_ID) %>%
  unique()

changed_export <- lake_dat %>%
  filter(Species_ID %in% changed_ids) %>%
  left_join(
    cmp %>%
      filter(Changed) %>%
      select(Species_ID, TI_obs, TI_est, Group_obs, Group_est),
    by = "Species_ID"
  ) %>%
  # optional: put the new columns at the end (delete if you don't care)
  relocate(TI_obs, TI_est, Group_obs, Group_est, .after = last_col()) %>%
  arrange(Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species)

write_csv(changed_export, "ChangedTaxa_LakeDat_WithObsEstTIandGroups.csv")

cat("Wrote: ChangedTaxa_LakeDat_WithObsEstTIandGroups.csv\n",
    "Rows (changed taxa): ", nrow(changed_export), "\n", sep = "")
```
